title: Revolutionary AI Cochlear Implant Brings New Hope to Hearing-Impaired Children in Rajasthan
description: A pioneering artificial intelligence-based smart cochlear implant was successfully implanted in a three-year-old girl at Rajasthan University of Health Sciences. This marks a significant advancement in government healthcare, potentially transforming the lives of hearing-impaired children across the state by enhancing auditory and speech development.
content: In a groundbreaking medical procedure, a three-year-old girl received an AI-based cochlear implant at the Rajasthan University of Health Sciences. This marks the first instance of such advanced technology being used in government hospitals in the sta... [624 chars]

title: Manu Joseph: Artificial intelligence isn’t funny and seems unable to grasp why that’s so
description: AI can write essays, poems and code, but ask it to be funny and it fails. Ask it why it isn’t funny and it fails again. What it doesn’t get is that humour is a form of human excellence—and that’s rare even among humans.
content: I have tried providing a setup and context. I gave ChatGPT my thoughts about this column and asked it to frame a humorous observation. It said: “Why did the AI cross the road? To optimize the chicken.” It is that bad. AI is better at word play than o... [5275 chars]

title: Hands tied, class 6 student swims across Vembanad Lake
description: Badri developed an interest in swimming at a young age and initially trained at the Udayanapuram Sree Muruga Swimming Club under retired Fire and.swimming with hands tied, Vembanad Lake swim, Vaikom boy swims lake, endurance swimming feat, young swimmer achieves, tied hands lake crossing, Badri R Nath swim record, AI powered swimming training, LLM for athletic performance analysis, student sets swimming milestone, swimming challenges overcome, artificial intelligence in sports, large language models for athletes, unique swimming stunts, Vembanad Lake open water swim
content:
Vaikom/Kottayam: In an impressive feat of endurance, Badri R Nath, a Class 6 student of Warwin School in Vaikom, swam across the Vembanad Lake with his hands tied.

title: Cleveland newspaper embraces use of AI
description: The Plain Dealer, Cleveland's largest newspaper, has begun to feature a new byline. On recent articles about an ice carving festival, a medical research discovery and a roaming pack of chicken-slaying dogs, a reporter's name is paired with the words "Advance Local Express Desk." It means: This article was drafted by artificial intelligence.
